critic_raw: The LSTM semiconductor failure prediction experiment uses a stratified random split on second-resolution sensor data with clustered failure events — creating severe temporal leakage. Degradation signatures in the hours before failure appear in both train and test sets under random split. Baseline feature engineering is unspecified. Maintenance schedule as confound control variable may introduce target leakage.
all_issues_raised: Temporal leakage from random split on high-frequency sensor data with clustered failure events
Baseline logistic regression feature engineering not specified
Maintenance schedule feature may introduce target leakage

round_transcripts.critic: Three issues: (1) Random split on second-resolution sensor data with clustered failures creates severe temporal leakage. (2) Baseline feature specification absent. (3) Maintenance schedule as confound variable risks target leakage.
round_transcripts.defender: Temporal leakage is the most serious concern — high-frequency sensor data with clustered failures makes random splits clearly inappropriate. A temporal split (train months 1-18, test months 19-24) is required. Baseline features must be enumerated with explicit temporal window constraints. Maintenance schedule feature inclusion requires causal analysis.

round_transcripts.critic: All three concerns conceded. Temporal leakage is potentially fatal to reported results.
round_transcripts.defender: Conceded. Three substantive changes required: temporal split, explicit baseline feature specification with temporal windows, causal analysis of maintenance schedule feature.

all_issues_adjudicated: Temporal leakage from random split on clustered high-frequency failure data — likely fatal without correction
Maintenance schedule feature may introduce target leakage
